summary: Defines the Chief AI Officer (CAIO) as a multifaceted role requiring a balance between technical experimentation, product strategy, and organizational coaching. It introduces a 20/60/20 Framework to help CAIOs manage these competing priorities based on the context of their organization's and individual's 'AI maturity'.
key_lesson: The Chief AI Officer must dynamically balance the roles of Tinker, Architect, and Coach using a framework that adjusts focus based on the context of current AI adoption.
evidence: Architect navigating the hype cycle to execute high-stakes product strategy
Coach guiding a workforce and your customers on meaningful AI adoption
push the sliders of focus based on your industry’s maturity and where you are
action_items: Apply a 20/60/20 Framework to balance technical experimentation, product strategy, and organizational coaching.
Assess your organization and own maturity in AI adoption.
